steps.label: Intake Channel Selection and Referral Initiation
steps.description: On this screen, you will begin a new intake referral. You will select the intake channel (Phone Call or Online Portal). The system will automatically generate a unique case ID and record the referral date and time. You will see a brief explanation of the intake process and what happens next. Please confirm the information before proceeding.

fields.label: Referral Date/Time
fields.error_message: Referral date and time is required.

data_validation: Referral date/time must be current and not in the future.
Case ID must be unique.

actions.name: Auto-Populate Referral Date/Time and Generate Case ID
actions.implementation: On screen load, the system automatically populates the current referral date/time and generates a unique case ID. These fields are displayed as read-only. The system also provides a brief explanation of the intake process and next steps.
actions.triggerCondition: Screen loaded.

actions.name: Validate Intake Channel and Proceed
actions.implementation: Validates that an intake channel is selected. If valid, proceeds to Intake Method Selection (STEP_2). If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen. Ensures referral date/time is current and case ID is unique.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and required fields are valid.

steps.label: Intake Method Selection
steps.description: On this screen, you will choose the intake method. If you select Conversational Intake, you will be able to enter a narrative and the system will pre-fill structured fields. If you select Standard Intake, you will draft the narrative during the call and it will be displayed for reference throughout the process. Instructions are provided to help you understand the differences.
data_validation: Intake method selection is required.

actions.name: Branch to Conversational Intake Narrative Entry
actions.implementation: If 'Conversational Intake' is selected, enables narrative entry and pre-fills structured fields where possible. Provides instructions and guidance on narrative entry. Proceeds to Narrative Entry (STEP_3).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and Intake Method is 'Conversational Intake'.

actions.name: Branch to Standard Intake Narrative Draft
actions.implementation: If 'Standard Intake' is selected, enables narrative draft entry during call and ensures the narrative will be displayed for reference throughout the process. Provides instructions and guidance. Proceeds to Narrative Draft (STEP_4).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and Intake Method is 'Standard Intake'.

steps.label: Narrative Entry (Conversational Intake)
steps.description: You will enter a detailed narrative describing the referral. The system will use this narrative to pre-fill structured fields in subsequent screens. Guidance is provided to help you include all relevant information. You can review and edit the narrative before continuing.
data_validation: Narrative description must not be empty.
Narrative should include all relevant referral details.

actions.name: Validate Conversational Narrative, Pre-Fill Structured Fields, and Proceed
actions.implementation: Validates that the narrative is entered. If valid, the system pre-fills structured fields in subsequent screens based on the narrative content. Provides guidance for narrative entry. Proceeds to Person Information Entry (STEP_5). If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and required narrative is present.

steps.label: Narrative Draft (Standard Intake)
steps.description: Draft the referral narrative during the call. The narrative will be displayed on each screen to assist with entering structured data. You can update the narrative as needed. Instructions are provided to help you capture all necessary information.
data_validation: Narrative draft must not be empty.
Narrative should be updated as new information is gathered.

actions.name: Validate Standard Narrative Draft, Display Narrative for Reference, and Proceed
actions.implementation: Validates that the narrative draft is entered. If valid, ensures the narrative is displayed on each subsequent screen to assist with structured data entry. Provides instructions for updating the narrative as new information is gathered. Proceeds to Person Information Entry (STEP_5). If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and required narrative draft is present.

steps.label: Person Information Entry
steps.description: Enter detailed information for each person involved in the referral. You can add multiple individuals. For each person, provide names, date of birth, language, address, contact details, gender, SSN, relationship to child(ren), intake role, school info/ICWA (if victim), and any special considerations. The system will generate a unique individual ID for each entry. Guidance is provided for each field.
data_validation: Birth date cannot be in the future.
SSN must be unique.
Cannot have more than one mother per child.
Phone number must be valid format.
If minor, guardian information is mandatory.
If Intake Role is Victim, school info/ICWA is required.
Relationship to child(ren) cannot be duplicated for the same person.

buttons.label: Add Person
buttons.description: Add another person to the referral.

actions.name: Add Person to Referral, Generate Individual ID, and Provide Field Guidance
actions.implementation: Validates entered person details. If valid, system generates a unique individual ID for the person, adds the person to the referral, clears fields for next entry, and provides guidance for each field. Allows multiple persons to be added. If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Add Person' clicked and all required person fields are valid.

actions.name: Proceed to Search Prior History
actions.implementation: Validates that at least one person has been added. If valid, proceeds to Search Prior History (STEP_6). If not, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and at least one person is present.

steps.label: Search Prior History
steps.description: The system will automatically search for prior history in criminal records, human services, courts, Medicaid, and internal databases. You will see a summary of matched records for each person entered. Review the results and confirm if any matches are relevant to the current referral.
data_validation: Matched person must correspond to entered person information.
Cannot confirm duplicate matches for the same person.

actions.name: Display and Confirm Review of Prior History Results
actions.implementation: System automatically searches for prior history in external and internal databases for each person entered. Displays a summary of matched records for each person. User reviews and confirms matches. Validates that the user has reviewed and confirmed the prior history results. If valid, proceeds to Review Fetched Data (STEP_7). If not, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and user has confirmed review of prior history results.

steps.label: Review Fetched Data
steps.description: Review the data fetched from prior history searches. Confirm matches and use the information to gain a better understanding of the case. You can mark records as relevant or not relevant. Instructions are provided to help you interpret the results.
data_validation: Cannot mark the same record as both relevant and not relevant.
Record type must match the source system.

actions.name: Mark Records as Relevant/Not Relevant and Proceed
actions.implementation: Displays fetched data from prior history searches. User marks records as relevant or not relevant. Validates that the user has reviewed and marked records appropriately. Provides instructions for interpreting results. If valid, proceeds to Duplicate Referral Check (STEP_8). If not, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and user has confirmed review of fetched data.

steps.label: Duplicate Referral Check
steps.description: Checks for duplicate referrals in the system. If a duplicate is found, the system prompts the Intake Case Worker to either link the referral to an existing record or end the intake process. If no duplicate is found, the intake process continues as a new entry.

rules.label: Duplicate Found
rules.logic: If the system identifies a referral with matching key attributes (e.g., person details, incident details) and status is 'error' with a relevant message, prompt the Intake Case Worker to link to the existing record or end the intake.

rules.label: No Duplicate Found
rules.logic: If no matching referral is found, or status is not 'error', allow the Intake Case Worker to proceed with creating a new referral entry.

actions.name: Duplicate Referral Detected - Prompt User
actions.implementation: If a duplicate referral is detected based on matching key attributes, system prompts the Intake Case Worker to link to an existing record or end the intake process. Proceeds to Duplicate Referral Handling (STEP_10).
actions.triggerCondition: Duplicate referral detected after prior history review.

actions.name: No Duplicate Referral - Proceed
actions.implementation: If no duplicate referral is detected, proceeds to Incident and Allegation Information Entry (STEP_11).
actions.triggerCondition: No duplicate referral detected after prior history review.

steps.label: Link to Existing Record or End Intake
steps.description: System links the referral to an existing case or person record if a duplicate is found, or terminates the intake process if the referral is not to be continued. The Intake Case Worker is responsible for confirming the match and selecting the appropriate action. The system must ensure data integrity when linking and must log the action taken.

assignments.label: Link to Existing Record
assignments.logic: If the Intake Case Worker confirms a duplicate referral, the system links the current referral to the identified existing case or person record using the provided IDs. The system updates the referral status and logs the linkage action.

assignments.label: End Intake
assignments.logic: If the Intake Case Worker determines the referral should not proceed (e.g., duplicate or erroneous entry), the system terminates the intake process for this referral, updates the status to 'ended', and logs the termination reason.

actions.name: Link Referral to Existing Record and Log Action
actions.implementation: If user selects 'Link to Existing Record', system links referral to existing case/person, updates status, logs action, and ends intake process. Displays confirmation and returns to dashboard or summary screen. Ensures data integrity and logs linkage action.
actions.triggerCondition: User selects 'Link to Existing Record' on STEP_10.

actions.name: End Intake Process and Log Reason
actions.implementation: If user selects 'End Intake', system terminates intake process, updates status to 'ended', logs reason, displays confirmation, and returns to dashboard or summary screen.
actions.triggerCondition: User selects 'End Intake' on STEP_10.

actions.name: Continue with New Entry
actions.implementation: If user selects 'Continue with New Entry', proceeds to Incident and Allegation Information Entry (STEP_11).
actions.triggerCondition: User selects 'Continue with New Entry' on STEP_10.

steps.label: Link to Existing Record or End Intake
steps.description: If a duplicate referral is found, you will be prompted to link this referral to an existing case/person or end the intake process. Review the information and make the appropriate selection. Guidance is provided to help you decide.

fields.label: Duplicate Referral Found
fields.error_message: Please indicate if a duplicate referral was found.

data_validation: If duplicate referral is found, action selection is required.
Cannot link to more than one existing record for the same referral.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to incident and allegation information entry.

actions.name: Handle Link to Existing Record with Guidance
actions.implementation: Triggers linking of referral to existing record via STEP_9.ACTION_9A. Provides guidance to help user decide. Displays confirmation and returns to dashboard or summary screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and user selected 'Link to Existing Record'.

actions.name: Handle End Intake with Guidance
actions.implementation: Triggers end of intake process via STEP_9.ACTION_9B. Provides guidance to help user decide. Displays confirmation and returns to dashboard or summary screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and user selected 'End Intake'.

actions.name: Handle Continue with New Entry with Guidance
actions.implementation: Triggers continuation of intake process via STEP_9.ACTION_9C. Provides guidance to help user decide. Proceeds to Incident and Allegation Information Entry (STEP_11).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and user selected 'Continue with New Entry' or 'No' for duplicate.

steps.label: Incident and Allegation Information Entry
steps.description: Enter detailed information about the incident and allegations. Provide a narrative, date/time, location, individuals present, injury details for each victim, law enforcement and medical provider involvement, fatality/near fatality status, substance-exposed infant, child removal, case type, abuse/allegation type, and safety risks. Instructions are provided for each field. You can add multiple victims and allegations.

fields.label: Law Enforcement Involvement
fields.error_message: Law enforcement involvement selection is optional.

fields.label: Medical Provider Involvement
fields.error_message: Medical provider involvement selection is optional.

data_validation: Incident date/time cannot be in the future.
At least one individual must be present.
If fatality or near fatality is selected, medical provider involvement is required.
Cannot have duplicate allegations for the same victim.
If child removal is selected, removal details must be provided.

buttons.label: Add Victim Injury
buttons.description: Add injury details for another victim.

actions.name: Add Victim Injury with Field Guidance
actions.implementation: Validates victim injury details and adds to the list. Provides instructions and guidance for each field. Allows multiple victim injuries to be added. If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Add Victim Injury' clicked and required fields are valid.

actions.name: Add Allegation with Field Guidance
actions.implementation: Validates allegation details and adds to the list. Provides instructions and guidance for each field. Allows multiple allegations to be added. If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Add Allegation' clicked and required fields are valid.

actions.name: Proceed to SDM Questionnaire with Instructions
actions.implementation: Validates that all required incident and allegation information is entered. Provides instructions for adding multiple victims and allegations. If valid, proceeds to SDM Questionnaire (STEP_12). If not, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and all required fields are valid.

steps.label: Structured Decision Making (SDM) Questionnaire
steps.description: Complete the SDM questionnaire to assess risk and recommend response time. Many fields will be pre-populated from previous screens. Review each question and provide additional information as needed. Guidance is provided for each question to help you make informed decisions.
data_validation: Risk level must be consistent with incident and allegation details.
Recommended response time must match risk level.
SDM narrative should support selected risk level and response time.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to disposition review and edit.

actions.name: Validate SDM Questionnaire, Pre-Populate Fields, Provide Guidance, and Proceed
actions.implementation: Validates SDM questionnaire responses. Pre-populates fields from previous screens. Provides guidance for each question to help user make informed decisions. If valid, proceeds to Disposition Review and Edit (STEP_13). If not, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Next' clicked and all required fields are valid.

steps.label: Disposition Review and Edit
steps.description: Review and edit the pre-populated intake type, allegations, victim demographics, exposure to perpetrator, risk level, and recommended response time. Select the disposition for the referral: 'screen in' or 'screen out'. Instructions are provided to help you make the correct selection.

fields.label: Allegations
fields.error_message: At least one allegation is required.

fields.label: Exposure to Perpetrator
fields.error_message: Exposure to perpetrator selection is required.

data_validation: At least one allegation must be selected.
Risk level must match SDM questionnaire.
If disposition is 'Screen In', recommended response time must be set.
Exposure to perpetrator must be specified for all victims.

actions.name: Submit Referral for Supervisor Review and Lock Editing
actions.implementation: Validates all required fields and submits referral for supervisor review. Locks the referral for editing after submission. If validation fails, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Submit for Supervisor Review' clicked and all validations pass.

steps.label: Submit Referral for Supervisor Review
steps.description: Upon submission, the system validates all required fields in the referral. If validation passes, the referral is locked for editing and routed to the supervisor's review queue. If validation fails, an error message is displayed and submission is blocked.

rules.label: Validation Success
rules.logic: If all required fields are present and status is not 'error', lock the referral for editing and route to supervisor queue.

rules.label: Validation Failure
rules.logic: If any required field is missing or status is 'error', display the error message and prevent submission until all required fields are completed.

actions.name: Validation Success - Lock and Route to Supervisor Queue
actions.implementation: If validation passes, locks referral for editing and routes to supervisor queue (STEP_15).
actions.triggerCondition: Referral submitted for supervisor review and all validations pass.

actions.name: Validation Failure - Show Error
actions.implementation: If validation fails, displays error message and prevents submission until all required fields are completed. Remains on current screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Referral submitted for supervisor review and validation fails.

steps.label: Assign to Supervisor
steps.description: Upon successful submission and validation of the referral by the Intake Case Worker, the system automatically assigns the referral record to the appropriate supervisor's review queue. The assignment is based on predefined routing logic (e.g., supervisor workload, region, or specialty). The system must ensure the referral is locked for editing and notify the supervisor.

assignments.label: Place Referral in Supervisor Queue
assignments.logic: System evaluates routing criteria and assigns the referral to the correct supervisor's review queue. The referral record is locked for further editing by the Intake Case Worker and a notification is sent to the assigned supervisor.

actions.name: Assign Referral to Supervisor and Notify
actions.implementation: Assigns referral to supervisor's review queue based on routing logic (e.g., workload, region, specialty). Locks referral for further editing by Intake Case Worker and sends notification to assigned supervisor. If assignment fails, displays error and remains on current screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Referral validated and ready for supervisor review.

steps.label: Supervisor Review Assignment
steps.description: As a supervisor, you will see referrals assigned to your review queue. Review the referral record and take appropriate action. You can view all details and select to approve or return for edits. Instructions are provided for each action.
data_validation: Referral record must be present in supervisor queue.
Review status must be updated upon action.

actions.name: Open Referral for Supervisor Review with Instructions
actions.implementation: Allows supervisor to open referral for detailed review (STEP_17). Provides instructions for each action (approve or return for edits).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Open Referral' clicked.

steps.label: Supervisor Review
steps.description: Review the submitted referral in detail. You can edit any field as needed and select to approve or return for edits. Guidance is provided to help you ensure all required information is present and accurate.
data_validation: All required referral fields must be completed before approval.
If returned for edits, comments must be provided.

buttons.label: Approve
buttons.description: Approve referral and proceed to finalize.

buttons.label: Return for Edits
buttons.description: Return referral to case worker for edits.

actions.name: Supervisor Approves Referral with Guidance
actions.implementation: Supervisor reviews referral and selects 'Approve'. Provides guidance to ensure all required information is present and accurate. Proceeds to supervisor approval decision (STEP_18.ACTION_18A).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Approve' clicked.

actions.name: Supervisor Returns Referral for Edits with Guidance
actions.implementation: Supervisor reviews referral and selects 'Return for Edits'. Provides guidance to ensure all required information is present and accurate. Proceeds to supervisor approval decision (STEP_18.ACTION_18B).
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Return for Edits' clicked.

steps.label: Supervisor Approval Decision
steps.description: Supervisor reviews the submitted referral and decides to approve or return for edits. If approved, the referral proceeds to finalization and assignment. If returned, the referral is sent back to the Intake Case Worker for required changes.

rules.label: Approved
rules.logic: If supervisor selects 'approve' and status is not 'error', proceed to finalize the referral and assign to the appropriate queue.

rules.label: Returned for Edits
rules.logic: If supervisor selects 'return for edits' or status is 'error', unlock the referral and notify the Intake Case Worker to make required changes.

actions.name: Finalize Referral After Approval
actions.implementation: If supervisor approves, proceeds to finalize referral (STEP_19).
actions.triggerCondition: Supervisor selects 'Approve'.

actions.name: Return Referral for Edits
actions.implementation: If supervisor returns for edits, unlocks referral and notifies case worker (STEP_20).
actions.triggerCondition: Supervisor selects 'Return for Edits'.

steps.label: Finalize Referral
steps.description: System marks the referral as finalized after supervisor approval and prepares it for assignment to the assessment/investigation queue. If an error occurs during finalization, the system displays an error message and prevents assignment.

rules.label: Finalize and Assign
rules.logic: If supervisor approval is received and status is not 'error', mark the referral as finalized and route to the assessment/investigation queue.

rules.label: Finalization Error
rules.logic: If status is 'error' during finalization, display the error message and prevent assignment until the issue is resolved.

actions.name: Finalize and Assign Referral
actions.implementation: If supervisor approval is received and finalization succeeds, marks referral as finalized and routes to assessment/investigation queue (STEP_22). Logs the assignment action. If error occurs, displays error and remains on current screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Supervisor approval received and finalization attempted, no error.

actions.name: Finalization Error - Show Error
actions.implementation: If error occurs during finalization, displays error message and remains on current screen for correction.
actions.triggerCondition: Supervisor approval received and finalization error occurs.

steps.label: Return Referral for Edits
steps.description: If the supervisor determines that edits are required, the system unlocks the referral for editing and notifies the Intake Case Worker. The referral status is updated to indicate it is pending edits, and the system logs the supervisor's comments or required changes.

assignments.label: Unlock Referral and Notify Case Worker
assignments.logic: System updates the referral status to 'pending edits', unlocks the record for editing, and sends a notification to the assigned Intake Case Worker with the supervisor's comments or required changes.

actions.name: Unlock Referral, Notify Case Worker, and Log Supervisor Comments
actions.implementation: Unlocks referral for editing, notifies Intake Case Worker with supervisor's comments, updates status to 'pending edits', and logs supervisor's comments or required changes. If notification fails, displays error and remains on current screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Supervisor returns referral for edits.

steps.label: Referral Returned for Edits
steps.description: Edit and update the referral as required after the supervisor returns it for edits. The system will unlock the referral and notify you of the required changes. Review supervisor comments and make the necessary updates before resubmitting.
data_validation: All supervisor comments must be addressed before resubmission.
Updated referral details must be complete and accurate.

buttons.label: Resubmit for Supervisor Review
buttons.description: Resubmit referral after making required edits.

actions.name: Resubmit Referral for Supervisor Review, Notify, and Log
actions.implementation: Validates that all supervisor comments are addressed and updated referral details are complete. Notifies Intake Case Worker of successful resubmission and logs supervisor's comments. If valid, resubmits referral for supervisor review by routing through validation (STEP_14) and assignment (STEP_15) before returning to supervisor queue. If not valid, displays error and remains on this screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Resubmit for Supervisor Review' clicked and all required fields are valid.

steps.label: Assign to Assessment/Investigation Queue
steps.description: After supervisor approval and finalization, the system routes the referral to the appropriate assessment or investigation queue. The routing is determined by intake type, disposition, and other relevant criteria. The system must update the referral status and log the assignment.

assignments.label: Route Referral to Designated Queue
assignments.logic: System evaluates the intake type, disposition, and assignment criteria to determine the correct assessment or investigation queue. The referral is assigned accordingly, status is updated, and the action is logged.

actions.name: Assign Referral to Assessment/Investigation Queue and Log Assignment
actions.implementation: Routes finalized referral to appropriate assessment/investigation queue based on intake type, disposition, and assignment criteria. Updates referral status and logs the assignment. If assignment fails, displays error and remains on current screen.
actions.triggerCondition: Referral finalized and ready for assignment.

steps.label: Assessment/Investigation Assignment
steps.description: As an assessment/investigation worker, you will see referrals assigned to your queue based on intake type and disposition. Review the referral details and begin the assessment or investigation process. Instructions are provided for next steps.
data_validation: Referral record must be present in assessment/investigation queue.
Assignment status must be updated as work progresses.

buttons.label: Open Referral
buttons.description: Open referral for assessment or investigation.

actions.name: Open Referral for Assessment/Investigation with Next Steps
actions.implementation: Allows assessment/investigation worker to open referral and begin process. Updates assignment status to 'In Progress'. Provides instructions for next steps as described in the technical data.
actions.triggerCondition: Button 'Open Referral' clicked.

actions.name: Update Assignment Status to Completed and Log
actions.implementation: Allows assessment/investigation worker to mark assignment status as 'Completed' when work is finished. Logs the completion action and updates status as described in the technical data.
actions.triggerCondition: Assignment work completed and status updated.
